title: TJU Toy Compiler (Rust-like → RV32IM)
summary: A modern C++26 compiler for a Rust-like language targeting RV32IM assembly. The pipeline is: preprocessor → lexer → parser → semantic analysis → IR generation (quadruples) → code generation. Lexer, parser, AST, type system, symbol table, and compiler driver are pre-implemented; the three subsystems that require implementation are semantic checker, IR builder, and the four-file RISC-V code-generation backend.
instructionPreview: This project is a Rust-like language compiler from an undergraduate compilers sequence, implemented in modern C++ (C++26), that targets RV32IM assembly. The compiler pipeline consists of: preprocessing, lexing, parsing, semantic analysis, IR (quadruple) generation, and RISC-V assembly code generation. The project is already present in `/workspace` and builds with `make all`.
instruction:
This project is a Rust-like language compiler from an undergraduate compilers sequence, implemented in modern C++ (C++26), that targets RV32IM assembly. The compiler pipeline consists of: preprocessing, lexing, parsing, semantic analysis, IR (quadruple) generation, and RISC-V assembly code generation. The project is already present in `/workspace` and builds with `make all`.

Your working directory is `/workspace`. The project code is already present.

Three major subsystems of the compiler have been left unimplemented (functions contain `/* TODO: implement */` stubs). You must implement all of them:

**1. Semantic Analysis (`src/semantic/semantic_checker.cpp`)**

The semantic checker performs type checking and validation during AST traversal using the CRTP visitor pattern. The following visit methods must be implemented:
- Function declaration checking (return type vs body type, missing return detection)
- Statement block expression checking (return coverage, type inference, auto-type checking)
- Variable declaration statement checking (type annotation vs initializer type, type inference, init checking)
- Expression statement checking (init checking, last-expression type propagation, used_as_stmt validation)
- Return expression checking (return type vs function return type, void return handling)
- Break expression checking (loop context validation, break-with-value in loop vs while/for, temp allocation)
- Assignment expression checking (mutability, type inference for unknown lval, init checking, type matching)
- Array access checking (base type validation, index type validation, element type extraction)
- Tuple access checking (base type validation, bounds checking, element type extraction)
- Function call checking (existence, argument count, argument type matching)
- If expression checking (condition type, branch type consistency, path coverage, missing else detection)
- While loop expression checking (condition type, body type validation)
- Loop expression checking (break type inference via BreakChecker, body type validation, temp value linkage)

Use the `ctx` (SemanticContext), `reporter` (error reporter), and helper methods `checkInit`/`checkCondition` already provided in the file. The `ReturnChecker` and `BreakChecker` classes are available for path coverage analysis.

**2. IR Generation (`src/ir/ir_builder.cpp`)**

The IR builder generates intermediate representation quadruples from the AST. The following visit methods must be implemented:
- Function declaration IR (implicit return insertion for unit-type functions, last-expression desugar for non-unit functions, concatenation of header/body/return IR)
- Statement block expression IR (symbol propagation from last expression, ircode extraction)
- Variable declaration statement IR (assignment quad for initialized variables)
- Return expression IR (with/without return value)
- Break expression IR (optional assignment + goto end label)
- Continue expression IR (goto start label)
- Assignment expression IR (assign quad from rval symbol to lval symbol)
- Array access and tuple access IR (temp allocation, access quad generation)
- Comparison and arithmetic expression IR (temp allocation, operation quad generation)
- Array element and tuple element IR (element extraction, MAKE_ARR/MAKE_TUP quads)
- Call expression IR (parameter preparation, call quad generation)
- ElseClause IR (conditional branch, if-expression value propagation, goto final label)
- If expression, while loop, for loop, range expression, iterable value, and loop expression IR

Use the `concatIrcode`, `extractIrcodeAndConcat`, `cmpOp2IROp`, `ariOp2IROp`, `extractSymbol`, `makeCondAndInsert`, `pushbackLabel`, and `insertLabels` helper functions and the `QuadFactory` for quad creation. The `ctx` (SemanticContext) provides scope, function name, and temp variable services.

**3. Code Generation (`src/codegen/`)**

The code generator translates IR quadruples to RISC-V assembly. Functions across four files must be implemented:

- `code_generate.cpp`: All emit methods for generating RISC-V instructions from IR operations including function prologue/epilogue, assignments, branches (beqz/bnez/bge), function calls, binary operations (register-register and immediate forms for add/sub/mul/div and all comparisons eq/neq/gt/geq/lt/leq), and constant folding.
- `stack_alloc.cpp`: Stack frame management including aligned allocation, sp movement with 16-byte alignment, scope enter/exit with marks, function entry (save ra) and return (restore ra, deallocate frame), and frame reset.
- `reg_alloc.cpp`: Register allocation with caller-first strategy, callee-saved register spilling on first use, symbol pool management (spill to stack, spill-except, spill-caller-saved, restore-callee-saved), round-robin spill eviction, and register freeing (by register and by symbol).
- `mem_alloc.cpp`: Memory allocation bridging register and stack allocators, including new/existing symbol allocation, register reuse, stack-to-register loading, argument allocation into a0-a7, and parameter preparation for function calls.

Refer to the RISC-V comparison instruction notes in `code_generate.cpp` for implementing EQ/NEQ/GT/GEQ/LT/LEQ using slt/sltiu/sltu/xor/xori. The allocator classes (`StackAllocator`, `RegAllocator`, `MemAllocator`) have their APIs defined in the corresponding headers.

Test programs under `test/` (especially `all_in_one.rs`, `1-1_2.rs`, `4-1_2.rs`, `5-1_3.rs`) exercise these code paths.

moduleDag.description: A modern C++26 compiler for a Rust-like language targeting RV32IM assembly. The pipeline is: preprocessor → lexer → parser → semantic analysis → IR generation (quadruples) → code generation. Lexer, parser, AST, type system, symbol table, and compiler driver are pre-implemented; the three subsystems that require implementation are semantic checker, IR builder, and the four-file RISC-V code-generation backend.

nodes.label: Semantic Checker
nodes.description: CRTP visitor that traverses the AST and enforces all type and control-flow rules: function return-type vs body-type, variable declaration and assignment type consistency, mutability, init-status tracking, array/tuple index validation, call argument compatibility, condition-type enforcement, if-branch consistency, break-value consistency, and return-path coverage via ReturnChecker/BreakChecker. Annotates each AST node with its resolved type, mutability, init flag, and the temp-value symbol that subsequent passes consume.

nodes.label: Stack Allocator
nodes.description: Manages the stack frame for a single function: lazily grows the frame in 16-byte blocks (emitting addi sp), saves/restores ra at function entry/exit, and maintains a scope-mark stack so that entering and exiting a lexical scope can reclaim stack space. All other code-gen components depend on it for physical memory addresses.

nodes.label: IR Builder
nodes.description: CRTP visitor that traverses the semantically-annotated AST and emits three-address quadruple IR. Desugars Rust-like constructs (implicit function returns, loop/break-with-value, for/range/iterable) into labeled basic blocks with goto, beqz, and bge quads. Reads the type and symbol annotations written by the semantic checker to produce correctly-typed temporaries and assignment quads.

nodes.label: Register Allocator
nodes.description: Caller-first, round-robin spill register allocator. Maintains a per-register symbol pool; evicts the oldest occupant when all registers are full. On first use of a callee-saved register it spills the saved value to stack (via StackAllocator) and restores it before function return. Exposes alloc, reuse, free-by-register, free-by-symbol, spillCaller, and restoreUsedCallee.

nodes.label: Memory Allocator
nodes.description: Bridges the IR symbol table to register and stack allocators. Tracks whether each live symbol is in a register or spilled to stack, loads stack-resident symbols on demand (emitting lw), handles register reuse for call return values, allocates argument registers a0–a7 at function entry, and prepares actual parameters before each call instruction.

nodes.label: Code Generator
nodes.description: Iterates over per-function IR quadruples and emits RV32IM assembly text. Implements emit methods for: function prologue/epilogue, assignment (li/mv), conditional branches (beqz/bnez/bge), function call with caller-save protocol, register-register and immediate arithmetic (add/sub/mul/div) and comparisons (eq/neq/gt/geq/lt/leq using slt/sltiu/sltu/xor/xori), and constant folding for all-constant binary expressions.

edges.label: IR builder reads type, symbol, and init-status annotations written by the semantic checker onto each AST node
RegAllocator calls stackalloc.alloc/spill/getFrameSize to spill callee-saved register values to the stack frame
MemAllocator calls stackalloc.offsetFromSP when emitting lw to reload a stack-resident symbol
MemAllocator calls regalloc.alloc/spillExcept/reuse/free for all register-level allocation and eviction
CodeGenerator calls memalloc.alloc/reuseReg/allocArgv/prepareParam to resolve every IR operand to a physical register
CodeGenerator translates IR quadruples produced by the IR builder; without valid IR there is nothing to emit
